source_snippets.TheForge.src.services.auth.session::File:
# File summary — /home/nmeyers/dev/claude/TheForge/src/services/auth/session.ts:1-24
/**
 * @file session.ts
 * @description Signed-cookie session for The Forge API.
 *
 * Issues a `forge_session` HttpOnly cookie on the first successful identity
 * resolution. Subsequent requests validate the cookie and skip Prisma entirely,
 * eliminating the "relog feeling" on every page load in development.
 *
 * Cookie: `forge_session=<HS256-JWT>`
 * JWT payload: `{ sub: actorId, email: string, role: string, iat, exp }`
 * TTL: 24 hours (hard-coded; not user-configurable in v1)
 *
 * Secret resolution (in order):
 *   1. `FORGE_SESSION_SECRET` env var
 *   2. `.forge/.session-secret` on disk (created + persisted if missing in dev)
 *   3. In production (NODE_ENV=production), absence of the env var throws at
 *      startup so the misconfiguration is caught early.
 *
 * Cookie is cleared on:
 *   - `POST /api/auth/logout`
 *   - Provider mismatch (actor changed between requests)
 *
 * Result-returning. Never throws.
 */
